purpose: An on/off switch that takes effect immediately: a light-DOM element wrapping a real <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> inside its own label — the APG switch pattern on a fully native input, drawn as a track with a traveling thumb.
useWhen: A setting applies the moment it changes, with no form submit (notifications on/off, dark mode).
The state reads as on/off rather than selected/unselected — screen readers announce it as a switch.

notWhen.case: The choice is collected and submitted with a form.
Exactly one choice among mutually exclusive options.

guidance: A toggle promises immediacy: flipping it must apply the change right away. If the change only lands on submit, that promise is broken — use zbk-checkbox.
Deliberately separate from zbk-checkbox so each carries an intentional token surface; there is no mixed/indeterminate state on a switch.

default.description: The label content (the accessible name via the wrapping label).
guidance: Label the setting, not the current state — "Email notifications", never "On".

keyboard.does: Flips the switch (native input behavior, preserved).

examples.title: State as label
examples.why: The switch role already announces on/off; a state word as the label reads as "On, switch, on" and goes stale the moment it flips. Name the setting instead.
